ncs_standard_definition.description_short: An Architectural Historian studies and interprets the historical development of buildings, monuments, and landscapes, analyzing their cultural, social, and economic contexts.
ncs_standard_definition.description_detailed: Architectural Historians work with governments, private companies, and non-profit organizations to preserve and protect cultural heritage sites, monuments, and buildings. They conduct research, write reports, and develop conservation plans to ensure the long-term preservation of these sites.

first_stepper.description: High school students interested in history, architecture, and culture can pursue a Bachelor's degree in History, Architecture, or a related field.

choice_maker.description: Individuals with a generic degree can pursue a Master's degree in Architectural History or a related field to gain specialized knowledge and skills.

future_builder.description: Architectural Historians with experience in the field can pursue advanced degrees, certifications, or specialized training to advance their careers.
future_builder.prerequisite_experience: 2-3 years of experience in heritage conservation

problem_solver_uturn.description: Individuals from unrelated fields can transition into Architectural History by acquiring relevant skills and experience.
